science.title: Supercomputer simulations map spliceosome motions in a two-million-atom human cell model
science.description: A new study from the Italian Institute of Technology (IIT), in collaboration with Uppsala University (Sweden) and AstraZeneca, shows how computational chemistry and supercomputers can help scientists better understand the fundamental mechanisms of life, specifically those of human cells. This research was conducted by the Molecular Modeling and Drug Discovery Unit, led by Marco De Vivo at IIT in Genoa, and was published in the journal Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences.

science.title: Massive star gamma-Cas found to be the source of powerful X-rays
science.description: After years of debate, researchers pinpoint the cause of gamma-Cas’s strange behavior to an unseen star consuming its material.

science.title: NASA Reveals Stunning New Radar View Of Mount Rainier Like Never Before

The Lyrids are active from the 15th through the 29th, with a pronounced maximum on the 22nd. science.title: Scandinavia's largest 'burial mound' may be a monument to catastrophe, not a king
science.description: New LiDAR analysis suggests Raknehaugen may have been built in response to a devastating landslide, not to honor a high-status individual. The study by Dr. Lars Gustavsen, published in the European Journal of Archaeology, challenges the long-held assumption that Scandinavia's largest prehistoric mound was built as a burial mound for a high-status individual.

ai.title: Google's AI isn't losing, it's just hiding the best features where nobody looks
ai.description: The company that "lost" the AI race is quietly winning a different one.

ai.title: The Wired Belts are the new Rust Belts: Report ranks which jobs are most vulnerable
ai.description: Digital Planet, the research center at the forefront of researching the AI transformation at The Fletcher School at Tufts University, today released the American AI Jobs Risk Index. It is a first-of-its-kind data-driven framework that maps the potential of AI-driven job vulnerability across every major occupation, industry, metropolitan area, and state in the United States. Drawing on 15 years of labor market data and the most current AI adoption research, the Index goes beyond prior studies by measuring actual vulnerability to job loss—not merely exposure—and connecting that vulnerability directly to projected income loss and geography.

ai.title: Gilles Arsenault removed from P.E.I. cabinet
